Interactions were examined on a D-phenylalanine-
modified surface, as well as on L-phenylalanine-modified surface. The ThT fluorescence of cross-aggregates on the L-Phe surface was weaker than that on D-Phe surface. 
The results clearly indicated surface chirality can strongly influence the structure, 
morphology, and cellular responses of the cross-aggregates of Aβ and insulin. On a D-Phe surface, Aβ induced insulin to undergo fibrillation and lose its bioactivity. In contrast, on an L-Phe surface, insulin and Aβ formed non-toxic amorphous aggregates, while insulin retained its native bioactivity and mitigated the Aβ-induced toxicity. This work offers new insights that may help us to understand insulin resistance in AD.

αS fibrils as interactor, Aβ monomers as interactee.The authors focused on studying inhibition, not accelerated aggregation. Therefore, they do not conclude that accelerated aggregation occurred as a result of the interaction of αS fibrils and Aβ monomers. I can see this on the ThT charts.

αS fibrils as interactor, Aβ oligomers as interactee. The authors focused on studying inhibition, not accelerated aggregation. Therefore, they do not conclude that accelerated aggregation occurred as a result of the interaction of αS fibrils and Aβ oligomers. I can see this on the ThT charts.

αS fibrils as interactor, Aβ fibrils as interactee. The authors focused on studying inhibition, not accelerated aggregation. Therefore, they do not conclude that accelerated aggregation occurred as a result of the interaction of αS fibrils and Aβ fibrils. I can see this on the ThT charts. The authors also investigate the effect of αS monomers / αS oligomers on Aβ fibrils. However, as  the authors' interpretation is not clear and it is difficult to read the kinetics from the ThT plots, I did not enter these interactions into the questionnaire.

Human α-Syn as interactor and  Mouse α-Syn as interactee. The authors claimed, that all examined fibrils are capable of seeding fibrils (so in Q2 maybe the answer should be "yes, implied by kinetics"). However, they also admit, that  initiation rates of cross-seeding reactions containing Human α-Syn were similar to unseeded reactions. No effect is also visible on ThT figure (Fig S2B), therefore I chose this answer in Q1.

catalyst. In solution, the C-terminal region of aSyn protects
aSyn against aggregation by its net negative charge density and
intramolecular interactions with the N-terminal region which
screen the amyloidogenic aSyn NAC region and prevent it
from aggregation (38). Based on our results, we hypothesize
that lysozyme seeds interact with the aSyn C-terminal region
resulting in less efficient screening of the amyloidogenic aSyn
NAC region, thus leaving aSyn in a more aggregation prone
conformation on the lysozyme fibril surface.

"We investigated the relationship between different assemblies of Aβ—freshly prepared, oligomeric, and fibrillar— and tau seeding using several cell-culture models, including FRET-based tau biosensor cells, human neuroblastoma cells, and primary hippocampal neurons from transgenic mice expressing human P301S tau."

Immunolabeling confirmed , that fibrils consist of two different amyloids, however there is no information about the structure of resulting fibrils. Therefore, the answer for 3rd question was "no information".

edges.question_2: Formation of fibrils by the interactee is inhibited
edges.general_remarks:
Interactee: Aβ42 - protofibrils and sonicated fibrils.
In general, Aβ40 inhibits Aβ42 aggregation. At Aβ40 monomers /Aβ42 protofibrils and Aβ40 monomers/Aβ42 fibrils ratios of 0.5 to 1, the formation of mature amyloid fibrils is inhibited, and predominantly protofibrillar and/or low ThT binding fibrils are formed.
Interactee: amyloid beta 42

Interactions were examined on a D-phenylalanine-
modified surface, as well as on L-phenylalanine-modified surface. The ThT fluorescence of cross-aggregates on the L-Phe surface was weaker than that on D-Phe surface. 
The results clearly indicated surface chirality can strongly influence the structure, 
morphology, and cellular responses of the cross-aggregates of Aβ and insulin. On a D-Phe surface, Aβ induced insulin to undergo fibrillation and lose its bioactivity. In contrast, on an L-Phe surface, insulin and Aβ formed non-toxic amorphous aggregates, while insulin retained its native bioactivity and mitigated the Aβ-induced toxicity. This work offers new insights that may help us to understand insulin resistance in AD.

The authors claimed, that all examined fibrils are capable of seeding fibrils, but these are inefficient (so in Q2 maybe the answer should be "yes, implied by kinetics"). However, they also admit, that initiation rates of cross-seeding reactions containing Mouse α-Syn WT were similar to unseeded reactions. No effect is also visible on ThT figure (Fig S3B), therefore I chose this answer in Q1.

The findings suggest that

The nucleation of aSyn fibrils is catalyzed by the presence of
lysozyme fibrils in which the lysozyme fibril surface acts as a
catalyst. In solution, the C-terminal region of aSyn protects
aSyn against aggregation by its net negative charge density and
intramolecular interactions with the N-terminal region which
screen the amyloidogenic aSyn NAC region and prevent it
from aggregation (38). Based on our results, we hypothesize
that lysozyme seeds interact with the aSyn C-terminal region
resulting in less efficient screening of the amyloidogenic aSyn
NAC region, thus leaving aSyn in a more aggregation prone
conformation on the lysozyme fibril surface.

edges.general_remarks: monomeric α-syn in the presence of 1 μM α-syn seed and addition of  oligomeric βH-crystallin. There were examined different concentrations of interactor. Here: 0.002, 0.01 mg/ml (while interactee concentration: 20 μM).  There wasn't mentioned by authors, if the interaction is resulted in fibirls. However they sometimes use word "retard" and sometimes "inhibit", what suggest they distinguish "slowing down" and "no aggregation" (it is also visible in ThT figures). Sequence of βH-crystallin was taken from uniprot (P07318), however it is a multi-subunit protein and the sequence isn't known by the authors.

We further found that the inhibition of Aß fibrillization by TDP-43 only occurs in the early and intermediate stages of Aß fibrillization, but not in the fibrillar stage and the seeding reaction. The result demonstrated that TDP-43 does not affect mature Aß fibrils and cannot block fibril growth once the fibril nucleus was added.
